summary: Phase 2 Authentication and RBAC testing complete. All 21 HTTP API tests passed. All 4 WebSocket auth tests passed. Supabase Auth is properly configured with three user roles (admin, operator, portfolio). Role-based access control correctly enforces HOW lens (operator/admin) and WHERE lens (portfolio/admin) separation.

minor.issue: Returns 403 instead of 401 for invalid credentials when Supabase returns 'Authentication failed' error. This is a minor inconsistency - the code checks for exact string 'Invalid credentials' to return 401.

critical_code_review_comments: Auth implementation is well-structured with proper separation of concerns (auth module, dependencies, service)
JWT tokens include role, organisation_id, and site_ids for scope-based access control
HOW/WHERE lens separation is correctly enforced at API level via FastAPI dependencies
WebSocket endpoints properly require token as query parameter and check lens access
Admin-only endpoints correctly use require_admin dependency
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - tests use existing Supabase users
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth tests created in /app/backend/tests/test_auth_rbac.py. All tests pass. Token caching implemented to avoid Supabase rate limits. WebSocket auth requires token as query parameter (?token=xxx). WebSocket connections work via localhost:8001.

websocket_auth_tests.test_ws_portfolio_cannot_access_prioriti: PASS - rejected with HTTP 403 (HOW lens required)

features_verified: Auth status endpoint GET /api/auth/status returns configured=true
Signin POST /api/auth/signin returns access_token for users with roles
Auth me GET /api/auth/me returns user info with role and lens access
HOW lens GET /api/how/priorities requires auth and HOW access
WHERE lens GET /api/where/priorities/summary requires auth and WHERE access
Operator can access HOW but not WHERE
Portfolio can access WHERE but not HOW
Admin can access both HOW and WHERE
Unauthenticated requests to protected routes return 401
WebSocket /ws/priorities requires auth token query parameter
